commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e95af5dfe2ebcd68b8d605004ab130569dffafe8)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds local auth marker helpers, filters generated and merged `models.json` provider rows by usable auth, and expands model allowlist error detail for agents/gateway sessions.

PR surface: Source +214, Tests +470. Total +684 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: no. live reproduction was run in this read-only review. Source inspection shows current main writes model-bearing rows on a broad `apiKey` check and runtime validation separately permits missing-key OAuth rows.
commands.review_followup: - The remaining blocker is an auth-provider compatibility decision that should be resolved by owners before automation changes the branch.

target.title: fix(agents): filter unresolved model registry rows
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #66961.

- Filter generated `models.json` provider rows before write so model-bearing providers only survive when they have a usable execution contract: `baseUrl` plus usable auth, or an explicit no-key auth mode such as `aws-sdk`.
- Keep resolved OAuth catalog rows only when the marker matches the provider, preserve supported local non-secret markers only on local/private endpoints, and drop stale remote/local/OAuth-looking markers.
- Improve the session/model-selection error for catalog-visible models blocked by `agents.defaults.models`.

## AI assistance disclosure

I used AI assistance for implementation, review, and proof collection. I reviewed and understand the affected path: generated `models.json` planning/merge, local auth marker resolution, model allowlist diagnostics, and the Gateway `sessions.patch` behavior.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: unresolved generated custom providers are excluded from `models.json`, supported local/OAuth/aws-sdk exceptions still work, and a catalog-visible but disallowed Codex model reports the allowlist fix.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, fake local `CODEX_API_KEY`, explicit loopback Gateway port/token.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
corepack pnpm exec tsx .artifacts/issue-66961-proof/run-proof.ts
```

Evidence after fix:

```text
$ openclaw models list --all --provider codex --plain
codex/gpt-5.4

$ ensureOpenClawModelsJson(loadConfig({pin:false}))
{"agentDir":"$PROOF_DIR/state/agents/main/agent","wrote":true}

$ inspect generated models.json
{
  "unresolvedPresent": false,
  "unresolvedOauthPresent": false,
  "oauthLocalLookingMarkerPresent": false,
  "amazonBedrockPresent": false,
  "amazonBedrockHasApiKey": false,
  "responsesLocalPreserved": true,
  "ollamaDockerPreserved": true,
  "lmstudioLocalPreserved": true,
  "singleLabelCustomLocalMarkerPresent": false,
  "remoteLocalMarkerPresent": false,
  "codexModels": [
    "gpt-5.4"
  ]
}

$ openclaw gateway run --bind loopback --auth token --allow-unconfigured
gateway_ready: true

$ backend callGateway sessions.patch {key, model}
{
  "ok": false,
  "name": "GatewayClientRequestError",
  "message": "model not allowed: codex/gpt-5.4 (model is listed in the catalog, but not allowed by agents.defaults.models; add \"codex/gpt-5.4\" or \"codex/*\" to agents.defaults.models)",
  "gatewayCode": "INVALID_REQUEST"
}
```

Observed result after fix: unresolved token/OAuth/non-env SecretRef providers are not written, blank `aws-sdk` auth stays keyless, matching `oauth:chutes` is preserved, local `custom-local`/`ollama-local`/`lmstudio-local` rows are preserved only on supported local endpoints, unsafe single-label/remote local-marker rows are dropped, and `sessions.patch` reports the concrete allowlist change needed for `codex/gpt-5.4`.

What was not tested: real provider calls, real OAuth refresh, real AWS SDK credential resolution, and remote provider network requests. The proof uses fake local credentials and exercises admission/model-selection behavior only.

## Root Cause

Generated and merged `models.json` rows could keep model-bearing providers that were not executable by the runtime registry. A non-empty `apiKey` field was treated too broadly, so unresolved auth modes, non-env SecretRef placeholders, stale OAuth markers, blank keys, and local no-auth markers attached to remote endpoints could survive into generated provider config.

The model registry requires model-bearing providers to have `baseUrl` and usable auth, except for explicit missing-key modes like `aws-sdk`. When invalid generated rows survived, they could poison catalog loading or produce confusing model-selection failures.

## Dependency contract

No external dependency contract changed.

Internal contracts checked:

- `src/agents/sessions/model-registry.ts` validates model-bearing provider rows with `baseUrl` and `apiKey`/OAuth, except allowed missing-key auth modes.
- `src/agents/models-config.providers.secrets.ts` can emit matching OAuth markers such as `oauth:chutes` after OAuth-backed provider auth is resolved.
- `extensions/ollama/src/discovery-shared.ts` treats `ollama-local` as the documented synthetic local key for local Ollama endpoints.
- `extensions/lmstudio/openclaw.plugin.json` declares `lmstudio-local` as a bundled non-secret local marker.

## Regression Test Plan

- Generated write path drops unresolved token/OAuth, stale OAuth-looking, non-env SecretRef, unsafe local-marker, and remote-local-marker providers.
- Generated write path preserves matching OAuth marker, `aws-sdk`, and supported local marker providers.
- Merge path drops stale invalid existing provider rows and preserves only self-contained existing rows.
- Runtime auth resolution normalizes supported local markers and rejects remote/non-local marker use.
- Gateway/session and cron model-selection tests cover the improved catalog-visible allowlist message.

## Security Impact

Positive: unresolved or stale credential markers are no longer persisted as executable model provider rows.

The local marker rule is intentionally conservative. It accepts known non-secret local markers only on loopback/private/local endpoints, IPv6 local ranges, known host-backed local aliases, or the documented Ollama service alias. It rejects OAuth marker syntax and arbitrary single-label OpenAI-compatible hosts.

## Human Verification

I reviewed the generated models path, auth marker predicate, model registry admission contract, model-selection diagnostics, focused tests, autoreview findings, `codex review` findings, and isolated Gateway proof output.

## CODEOWNERS / owner review

Touched auth files match `.github/CODEOWNERS` entries for `@openclaw/openclaw-secops`:

- `/src/agents/*auth*.ts`
- `/src/agents/**/*auth*.ts`

Expected owner review: `@openclaw/openclaw-secops`.

## Changelog

No `CHANGELOG.md` edit. This is a contributor fix; release-note context is in this PR body.

## Review conversations

- Autoreview found an OAuth-marker over-admission issue for markers ending in `-local`; fixed by excluding colon marker syntaxes from local marker classification.
- Autoreview found arbitrary single-label host over-admission; fixed by narrowing the exception to the documented `ollama` service alias.
- `codex review --disable image_generation --base origin/main` previously found that matching OAuth markers must be preserved; fixed by accepting only `oauth:<providerId>` for the same provider.
- Final autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.
- Final `codex review --disable image_generation --base origin/main`: clean, no actionable correctness issues.

## Risks

- The local-marker predicate still needs maintainer judgment for future plugin-owned non-secret markers. The current rule is conservative and driven by bundled local provider contracts.

## Behavior tradeoffs

- Matching `oauth:<providerId>` is treated as usable generated auth evidence for that provider; stale or mismatched OAuth markers are dropped.
- Arbitrary single-label OpenAI-compatible hosts are not treated as local no-auth endpoints. Users with non-standard hostnames can provide real credentials or use a supported local/private endpoint form.

## Scope boundaries

- Does not change `agents.defaults.models` allowlist semantics.
- Does not add runtime compatibility for non-env SecretRefs in generated `models.json`.
- Does not change provider discovery, provider calls, OAuth refresh, AWS SDK auth, or plugin registry behavior.
- Does not edit docs or changelog.

## Validation

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.merge.test.ts src/agents/models-config.skips-writing-models-json-no-env-token.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.model-formatting.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize-keys.test.ts src/agents/models-config.runtime-source-snapshot.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-resolve.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.model.test.ts src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts src/agents/models-config.uses-first-github-copilot-profile-env-tokens.test.ts
corepack pnpm tsgo:core && corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test
corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/model-auth-local.ts src/agents/model-auth.ts src/agents/model-auth.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-shared.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.ts src/agents/models-config.merge.test.ts src/agents/models-config.merge.ts src/agents/models-config.plan.ts src/agents/models-config.skips-writing-models-json-no-env-token.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent.model-formatting.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/model-selection.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts
git diff --check
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local --base origin/main
codex review --disable image_generation --base origin/main
corepack pnpm exec tsx .artifacts/issue-66961-proof/run-proof.ts
```
target.author: ferminquant

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
